title: PLD Space secures $35 million European Investment Bank loan for MIURA 5 launcher
summary: PLD Space secured a $35 million loan from the European Investment Bank to fund the final development of its MIURA 5 lightweight rocket. The MIURA 5 is designed to deploy payloads of up to 1 ton to low Earth orbit.

title: Heather Pringle on the upcoming Space Symposium and future international collaboration in space
summary: Heather Pringle, CEO of a major space organization, discusses upcoming international collaboration in space at the Space Symposium. The interview highlights future prospects for global partnerships in the space industry.

title: China conducts pair of Long March launches for Thousand Sails and Guowang megaconstellations
summary: China launched two Long March rockets (Long March 8 and Long March 6A) to deploy satellites for its Thousand Sails and Guowang megaconstellations. These missions expand China's satellite networks for communications and other applications.

title: A new kind of arsenal: commercial satellites
summary: The Pentagon is shifting its strategy to rely on commercial satellites for future conflicts, recognizing their growing importance. This marks a shift from questioning the role of commercial space to actively integrating it into military planning.

title: What returning to lunar space means for human health
summary: Returning to the lunar surface poses significant health risks for astronauts, some of which are subtle and cumulative. The upcoming missions aim to address these challenges after the last human lunar landing in 1972.

title: Portal Space Systems raises $50 million to accelerate spacecraft development
summary: Portal Space Systems secured $50 million in funding to advance the development of its highly maneuverable spacecraft. The space mobility company aims to scale up its operations with the new capital.

title: Space Force taps 14 firms for $1.8 billion GEO surveillance program
summary: The U.S. Space Force has selected 14 companies to compete for $1.8 billion in contracts to develop 'neighborhood watch' satellites for GEO surveillance. This initiative aims to enhance space domain awareness and monitoring capabilities.

title: Albedo prepares second VLEO mission for 2027 launch
summary: Albedo, a satellite manufacturing company, plans to launch its second very low-Earth orbit (VLEO) spacecraft, Vicinity, in 2027. The new satellite will feature solar arrays for significantly higher power output compared to its predecessor, Precision, launched in 2025.

title: Lockheed Martin wins $105 million contract for GPS ground control system as OCX winds down
summary: Lockheed Martin secured a $105 million contract to develop a GPS ground control system for next-generation GPS IIIF satellites, replacing the aging OCX system. The contract extends support through 2030.

title: Vantor to expand imaging satellite fleet, adding smallsats to increase revisit rates
summary: Vantor plans to expand its imaging satellite fleet by adding small satellites to enhance revisit rates. The new architecture aims to combine high resolution with rapid revisit capabilities.

title: The Artemis 2 astronauts got a private 'Project Hail Mary' screening before launch. Here's their verdict
summary: The Artemis 2 astronauts attended a private screening of 'Project Hail Mary' before their upcoming launch. They praised the movie as uplifting and inspiring.

title: Crew of tiny worms readies for April 11 launch to International Space Station
summary: A crew of tiny worms is set to launch to the International Space Station on April 11 to study the effects of long-term spaceflight on human astronauts. The six-month mission aims to provide insights into human health in space.

title: 'It probably will start a precedent': Why satellite company's withholding of Iran imagery has this expert worried
summary: Planet Labs has decided to withhold satellite imagery of Iran and the Middle East in response to a U.S. government request, raising concerns about setting a precedent for censorship in satellite data sharing.

title: A house-size asteroid is heading toward Earth tonight: Here's what you need to know
summary: A house-size asteroid is approaching Earth tonight but poses no threat, according to the European Space Agency's Risk List.

title: Watch Artemis 2 race back to Earth in this telescope livestream tonight
summary: A Virtual Telescope Project livestream will track NASA's Artemis 2 Orion spacecraft as it returns to Earth. The event can be watched live tonight.

title: 1 week until the Lyrid meteor shower lights up April skies: Here's what you need to know
summary: The Lyrid meteor shower is set to peak overnight on April 21-22, offering a celestial spectacle in the April skies. This annual event provides an opportunity for stargazers to observe shooting stars.

title: NASA astronaut Leland Melvin makes a cartoon cameo in latest episode of Disney+'s 'Hey A.J.!'
summary: NASA astronaut Leland Melvin appeared in a cartoon cameo on Disney+'s 'Hey A.J.' show, sharing an inspirational message about dreaming big. He emphasized that perseverance can lead to extraordinary achievements.

title: Artemis 2 astronaut poses for epic selfie | Space photo of the day for April 9, 2026
summary: Astronaut Christina Koch poses for a selfie aboard the Artemis 2 Orion spacecraft, featuring a zero-gravity indicator named 'Rise'. The image was highlighted as the space photo of the day for April 9, 2026.

title: 'We all pretty much broke down right there': Inside the Artemis 2 astronauts' emotional moment near the moon
summary: The Artemis 2 astronauts experienced an emotional moment near the Moon after breaking the human spaceflight distance record, strengthening their bond. This milestone highlights progress in NASA's Artemis program.

title: Light pollution has brightened Earth by 16% since 2014, satellites find
summary: A new study using satellite images found that artificial night lights have brightened Earth by 16% from 2014 to 2022, increasing light pollution globally.

title: Earthset From the Lunar Far Side
summary: NASA's Artemis II mission crew captured stunning images of Earth from the far side of the Moon during their lunar journey. This marks a significant milestone in lunar exploration and public engagement.

title: Indoor Testing Facilities available at the NASA Unmanned Autonomy Research Complex (NUARC)
summary: NASA's Unmanned Autonomy Research Complex (NUARC) offers indoor testing facilities, including a WindShaper fan array for dynamic low-speed and hovering flight research, and a WindProbe for flow surveys using motion capture technology.

title: I Am Artemis: Dan Florez
summary: Dan Florez, test director for NASA's Exploration Ground Systems Program, describes witnessing the Artemis I launch from Kennedy Space Center's Launch Control Center on November 16, 2022. The event marked a significant milestone for NASA's Artemis program.

title: Indoor Testing Facilities available at the NASA Unmanned Autonomy Research Complex (NUARC)
summary: NASA's Unmanned Autonomy Research Complex (NUARC) offers a WindShaper fan array for dynamic low-speed and hovering flight research, capable of generating arbitrary wind gradients and gusts via a Python API. A companion WindProbe uses OptiTrack motion capture to survey flows.

title: Starstruck
summary: The Artemis II crew captured a photo of the Milky Way galaxy on April 7, 2026, showcasing its spiral structure with two arms extending from a central bar of stars. The galaxy spans over 100,000 light-years, with Earth located along one of its spiral arms.

title: Watching the Artemis II Mission Unfold at JPL’s Space Flight Operations Facility
summary: NASA's Jet Propulsion Laboratory staff will monitor the Artemis II mission from the Space Flight Operations Facility in Southern California after its planned launch on April 1, 2026. The Deep Space Network (DSN), operated from this facility, consists of three complexes in Goldstone, Madrid, and Canberra.

title: The Deep Space Network Acquires Artemis II Signal
summary: NASA's Deep Space Network successfully acquired a signal from the Artemis II mission to the Moon on April 1, 2026. The signal was detected using antennas at the Madrid Deep Space Communications Complex.

title: The Deep Space Network Acquires Artemis II Signal
summary: NASA's Deep Space Network (DSN) successfully acquired the radio frequency signal from the Artemis II crewed Moon mission shortly after its launch on April 1, 2026. The DSN's tracking capabilities were demonstrated by a peak in the data signal on a computer screen.

title: Watching Over the Deep Space Network Before Artemis II Signal Acquisition
summary: NASA's Blanca Renteria monitored data at the Space Flight Operations Facility during the Artemis II launch on April 1, 2026. The facility operates the Deep Space Network (DSN) to support the mission.

title: Watching the Artemis II Launch From JPL’s Space Flight Operations Facility
summary: NASA's Jet Propulsion Laboratory staff will monitor the Artemis II mission launch to the Moon from the Space Flight Operations Facility. The crew will communicate with the Near Space Network shortly after liftoff.

title: Artemis II closing in on Earth return
summary: The Artemis II mission, which marked a historic 10-day journey carrying humans farther from Earth than ever before, is nearing its return to Earth. The mission is a significant milestone for NASA's Artemis program.

title: China debuts new launchers, tests orbital servicing and outlines future deep-space missions
summary: China has debuted new commercial launchers from CAS Space and Space Pioneer, while also testing orbital servicing and outlining future deep-space missions.

title: SpaceX continues refinements at Starbase Pad 2 ahead of Booster 19 return
summary: SpaceX is making final preparations at Starbase Pad 2 for the return of Booster 19. The refinements are part of ongoing operations at the facility.

title: Artemis II breaks record, conducts lunar flyby
summary: Artemis II achieved a record-breaking lunar flyby with the Orion spacecraft Integrity, carrying astronauts Reid Wiseman, Victor Glover, Christina Koch, and Jeremy Hansen. The mission marks a significant milestone in NASA's Artemis program.

title: Launch Preview: Falcon 9 to loft Cygnus to ISS, Minotaur IV to launch from California
summary: A Falcon 9 rocket is set to launch a Cygnus spacecraft to the International Space Station (ISS), while a Minotaur IV rocket is scheduled to launch from California. This comes as NASA's Artemis II mission approaches its halfway point.

title: Orion Spacecraft Races Toward Historic Lunar Flyby in Artemis II Mission
summary: NASA's Artemis II mission is in a critical mid-flight phase as the Orion spacecraft approaches a historic lunar flyby. This marks a significant milestone for the mission, which aims to advance lunar exploration.

title: Artemis II Orion performs TLI Burn — Now headed to the Moon
summary: NASA's Orion spacecraft successfully performed the Trans-Lunar Injection (TLI) burn, marking a historic milestone as it heads toward the Moon. This achievement is part of the Artemis II mission, the first crewed lunar mission in over 50 years.

title: Artemis II returns humanity to the Moon with launch on Wednesday evening
summary: The Artemis II mission aims to return humanity to the Moon with a launch scheduled for Wednesday evening. This marks a significant milestone in space exploration following the Apollo 17 mission in 1972.

title: Launch Preview: SLS, Falcon 9, Atlas V, and Soyuz launches comprise busy launch manifest
summary: Nine launches are scheduled this week, including SLS, Falcon 9, Atlas V, and Soyuz rockets, lifting off from Florida, California, and Russia.

title: America’s Rocket Factory comes Full Circle with Artemis
summary: America's Rocket Factory is playing a key role in NASA's Artemis program, marking a significant milestone in the return of crewed launches beyond low Earth orbit. The collaboration highlights the growing importance of private space companies in NASA's deep space missions.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: TEC Business to Business website reported on an unspecified space industry news. No specific details were provided in the given text.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) released a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements over its 50-year history, marking significant milestones in space exploration and technology.

title: Graphene and lasers for space propulsion
summary: An international research team demonstrated light-based propulsion using graphene aerogels during ESA's parabolic flight campaign in May 2025. The study, published in Advanced Science, showed that microgravity significantly enhances propulsion efficiency compared to Earth's gravity conditions.

title: Graphene and lasers for space propulsion
summary: Graphene and lasers could revolutionize space propulsion by steering solar sails and adjusting satellite positions. An experiment on a gravity rollercoaster demonstrated the potential of this innovative material.

title: A silicon chip that can do it all
summary: A breakthrough silicon chip has been developed that integrates multiple functionalities, potentially revolutionizing space electronics. This innovation could enhance satellite and spacecraft systems by reducing size and power consumption.

title: Proba-3’s Coronagraph is alive!
summary: The Proba-3 mission's Coronagraph spacecraft has re-established contact with ground control after a month-long anomaly. The mission team confirmed the spacecraft is operational and communicating again.

title: Upcoming ESA–Industry events on CM25 implementation
summary: ESA is hosting a series of events to present the follow-up to the Council Meeting at Ministerial Level 2025 (CM25) for the industrial community. These events aim to inform about planned activities, priorities, and opportunities across ESA programs.

title: Plato readies for space-like tests
summary: ESA's Plato mission, designed to discover Earth-like exoplanets, is undergoing critical space-like tests in the Large Space Simulator (LSS) chamber. The spacecraft, equipped with 26 ultrasensitive cameras, is set for a January 2027 launch on an Ariane 6 rocket.

title: Former NASA astronaut Jeanette Epps visits ESTEC
summary: Former NASA astronaut Jeanette Epps visited ESTEC, the European Space Agency's technical center. This visit highlights international collaboration in space exploration.

title: Portal Closes $50M Series A
summary: Portal has closed a $50 million Series A funding round led by Geodesic Capital and Mach33, with additional investment from Booz Allen Ventures, Ark Invest, AlleyCorp, and FUSE.

title: The State of Launch 2026
summary: Rocket Lab's VP of launch highlights how deadlines in programs like Golden Dome can determine winners and losers in the space industry. Constraints around launch capabilities may impact eligibility to compete.

title: Vantor Unveils New Sat Classes: Vantage and Pulse
summary: Vantor introduced two new satellite classes, Vantage and Pulse, aimed at enhancing imagery resolution and revisit rate. The announcement highlights the company's focus on advancing its satellite technology capabilities.

title: The State of ISAM 2026
summary: The article discusses the growing complexity of In-Space Servicing, Assembly, and Manufacturing (ISAM) as it evolves beyond a single concept. It highlights the need to redefine ISAM as a multifaceted field.

title: Czech Radiation Chips on Artemis II Bring ISS Experience
summary: Czech-made radiation chips will be part of the Artemis II mission, leveraging ISS experience to monitor solar flares during solar maximum. This initiative aims to enhance space radiation detection capabilities for future missions.

title: Advocates Ready For NASA Science Funding Fight, Part II
summary: Advocates and lawmakers are opposing the Trump administration's proposed $18.8 billion NASA budget, which includes significant cuts to science and STEM missions. They vow to fight against the reductions to protect key programs.

title: Starfish Closes $100M+ Series B
summary: Starfish Space has secured over $100 million in a Series B funding round led by Point72 Ventures, with additional participation from Activate Capital and Shield Capital.

title: The State of EO 2026
summary: The Earth Observation (EO) industry is experiencing significant growth as more organizations seek to deploy their own satellite capabilities. This trend is expected to continue and expand by 2026.

title: How Earthlings Got Views of the Lunar Fly By
summary: Redwire provided 11 cameras for the Orion capsule, enabling live views of the lunar flyby during the Artemis mission. These cameras were installed both inside and outside the spacecraft.

title: The State of Satcom 2026
summary: SpaceX and Amazon are disrupting the satcom market, forcing traditional players and startups to pivot and find innovative ways to compete. The crowded market is pushing companies to explore new strategies to stay relevant.

title: Network Integration Remains a Challenge in Pushing Data to the Tactical Edge
summary: Commercial space leaders are working to push data from centralized nodes to the tactical edge for faster decision-making in conflicts. Network integration remains a significant challenge in this effort.

title: Vantor Introduces New Satellite Classes for High Resolution Imaging, Frequent Revisits
summary: Vantor launched two new classes of electro-optical imaging satellites designed for high-resolution imagery and frequent revisit rates. This innovation aims to enhance commercial satellite operations and spatial intelligence services.

title: Space Force Awards 14 Companies Space Domain Awareness Contracts Under “Andromeda”
summary: The U.S. Space Force awarded 14 companies over $1.8 billion in contracts for space domain awareness under the 'Andromeda' program. The contracts, managed by Space Systems Command, aim to enhance reconnaissance capabilities in geosynchronous orbit.

title: NSG Names Haithem Alfaraj as its Second CEO
summary: Neo Space Group (NSG), a Saudi space company, has appointed Haithem Mohammed Mojil Alfaraj as its new CEO, succeeding Martijn Blanken who led the company since its inception.

title: In-Space Maneuverability Startup Portal Raises Series A as it Shifts into Production Mode
summary: Portal Space Systems raised $50 million in a Series A funding round to accelerate the development of maneuverable spacecraft and expand its manufacturing capacity. The startup is transitioning into production mode as it grows its team.

title: Live in the Booth: Sky Perfect JSAT President & CEO Eiichi Yonekura
summary: Sky Perfect JSAT, a major global satellite operator, is focusing on a multi-faceted strategy to expand its business in the coming years. The company's President & CEO, Eiichi Yonekura, discussed these plans in a recent interview.

title: FCC to Vote on Order to Update EPFD Framework
summary: The FCC is preparing to vote on an order to update spectrum-sharing rules between Geostationary (GEO) and Non-Geostationary (NGSO) satellite systems. The proposed changes aim to replace the existing EPFD framework.

title: Italy and Argentina to Enhance Space Collaboration With Telespazio and VENG Deal
summary: Italy and Argentina have agreed to enhance their space collaboration through a new multi-year deal between e-GEOS (a joint venture of the Italian Space Agency and Telespazio) and VENG. The agreement focuses on advancing space data applications.

title: Northrop Grumman to Build HUGEO Satellite for Hungary’s 4iG
summary: Northrop Grumman has signed a deal with Hungary's 4iG Space and Defense Technologies to build the nation's first communications satellite, named HUGEO. The agreement marks a significant step for Hungary's space capabilities.

title: April/May Magazine: Space as Critical Infrastructure
summary: Via Satellite's April/May magazine focuses on space as critical infrastructure and cybersecurity challenges for lunar activities. It also covers Europe's ramp-up in space defense.
